title: Number()
subtitle: Converting to a number
goal: Learn how to convert values to numbers using Number().
explanation: Number() converts a value to the number type.
It is most often used to convert text entered by the user.
If a string contains a valid number, the conversion succeeds.
After conversion, you can perform math operations on the value.
If conversion is not possible, JavaScript returns NaN.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: Imagine a card with "42" written on it. Number() reads the text and turns it into the actual number 42.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Beginners often think Number() works with any text. For example, Number("Hello") returns NaN.
